---
title: "Empirical cross-entropy minimization for model fitting"
format: html
---

## Simulate data

```{r}
set.seed(666)

n <- 100

x1 <- rnorm(n, mean = 5, sd = 2)
x2 <- rnorm(n, mean = -2, sd = 1.5)

# true parameters
true_b0 <- 3.0
true_b1 <- 1.5
true_b2 <- -2.5
true_sigma <- 1.8

y <- true_b0 + (true_b1 * x1) + (true_b2 * x2) + rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = true_sigma)
```

## Specify loss function (empirical cross-entropy)

```{r}
xh <- function(params, x1, x2, y) {
  b0    <- params[1]
  b1    <- params[2]
  b2    <- params[3]
  log_sigma <- params[4] # log(sigma) trick to avoid issues with the optimizer
  
  mu <- b0 + (b1 * x1) + (b2 * x2)
  
  sigma <- exp(log_sigma)
  
  log_q <- dnorm(y, mean = mu, sd = sigma, log = TRUE)
  
  # empirical cross-entropy: -1/N * sum(log(q(y | x)))
  loss <- -mean(log_q)
  return(loss)
}
```

## Fit model to data

```{r}
par <- c(0, 0, 0, 0) # log(sigma) = 0 -> sigma = 1

xh_fit <- optim(
  par = par,
  fn = xh,
  x1 = x1,
  x2 = x2,
  y = y,
  method = "BFGS"
)
```

## Empirical cross-entropy fit

```{r}
cat("Intercept (b0):", xh_fit$par[1], "\n")
cat("Slope X1 (b1): ", xh_fit$par[2], "\n")
cat("Slope X2 (b2): ", xh_fit$par[3], "\n")
cat("Sigma:         ", exp(xh_fit$par[4]), "\n")
```

## Standard OLS fit

```{r}
ols_fit <- lm(y ~ x1 + x2)
coef(ols_fit)
sigma(ols_fit)
```
